============================================================
RAW DATA NOT FOUND
============================================================
Expected file: /workspace/raw_data/retraction_data.csv
No reproduction possible – printing paper-reported values only.

REQUIRED DATA:
- Article-level file with columns:
  * gender_first_author   : 'M' or 'F' (or 'unknown')
  * retracted             : 1 for retracted, 0 for non-retracted
  * publication_year      : 2008-2023
  * retraction_reason     : string (e.g., 'plagiarism','mistakes', etc.)
    (multiple reasons may be separated, handled accordingly)
  * subject_field         : one of the 5 Leiden Ranking fields
  * country_first_author  : standard country name/ISO code
  * [optional] gender_corresponding_author

All results below marked as PAPER_REPORTED.

RESULT PAPER_REPORTED overall_RR_male = 6.38 per 10,000
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED overall_RR_female = 5.19 per 10,000
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED overall_MFRR = 1.23 (95% CI: 1.18, 1.28)
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED MFRR fluctuates between 1.0 and 1.5, mostly >1 (2008-2023).
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_plagiarism = 1.99
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_authorship_issues = 1.73
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_duplication ~ >1 (smaller)
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_fabrication_falsification ~ >1 (smaller)
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_ethical_issues ~ >1 (smaller)
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED MFRR_mistakes = not significant
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED Biomedical & health sciences: male > female
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED Life & earth sciences: male > female
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED Physical sciences & engineering: male > female
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED Mathematics & computer science: female > male
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED Social sciences & humanities: no significant difference
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED Iran, Pakistan, USA: male higher retraction rate
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED Italy, China: female higher retraction rate
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED Other top 10 countries: no significant difference
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED overall_MFRR_corresponding = 1.20 (95% CI: 1.15, 1.25)
RESULT PAPER_REPORTED Consistency with first-author analysis: yes.

CONCLUSION: Male leading authors generally have higher retraction rates than females.
However, gender disparities vary by retraction reason, discipline, and country.
